extraction.one_line_summary: YUAN Caleb won bunch sprint after staying sheltered behind leadout while SAGAN forced to hit wind early from 800m, VAN POPPEL nearly closed in final 50m.
tactical_patterns: SAGAN teams controlled despite third GC position, actively riding for sprint bonus seconds not overall protection revealing stage race priorities.
YUAN held pocket 200m longer than SAGAN who hit wind from 800m; positioning timing differential decided sprint not pure power.
VAN POPPEL nearly equalized final 50m after appearing distanced, suggesting YUAN's smaller frame created optical illusion of larger winning margin.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: throwing for bonus seconds at line despite yellow jersey and third GC shows prioritizing stage hunting over conserving energy for mountains.
rider_signals.YUAN Caleb: positive: first win since Giro d'Italia ends drought, needed confidence boost as head drops without results per commentary.
rider_signals.HAIG Jack: positive: doing full leadout work with only 6 Orica riders remaining after BEWLEY DNS reveals team depth under attrition.
rider_signals.PORSEV Alexander: negative: 14th place disappointing for expectations, significantly underperformed against similar fast finisher profile.
